text.title: Python Type Hints Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree mapping Python version + repo state to a typing-rollout configuration. Leaves reference rules from 01-core-rules.xml.

decision-tree.root-question: What Python version does `pyproject.toml requires-python` declare?

conclusion.verdict: abort; methodology requires 3.9+ for `list[T]` and 3.10+ for `X | None`. Upgrade Python before adopting.
`list[T]` available; `X | None` requires `from __future__ import annotations` (caveat: Pydantic v2 only). Use `Optional[X]` if v1 still in use.
mode=greenfield; `strict = true` globally; ban bare ignores via ruff PGH003
mode=legacy; `files = [public-API allowlist]`; grow per PR; CI runs strict only on changed files

branch.question: Is this repo a PyPI-published library?

conclusion.verdict: mode=library; `disallow_any_generics = true`; use PEP 695 `def f[T]()` for new generics; document Protocol contracts publicly
mode=greenfield or legacy depending on coverage; PEP 695 generic syntax allowed in new files

text.example: python_version=3.11 → q-coverage: coverage=35% → mode=legacy → `[tool.mypy] files=[src/api/handlers.py]` (seed with public API). PR touches `src/api/handlers.py` → CI runs `mypy --strict` on that file only → green. Each new typed file gets appended to `files`. Repo-wide percentage never blocks PRs.
